input.new_string:
"/tenants/{id}":
    get:
      tags:
      - Tenants
      summary: Show tenant
      operationId: tenantsShow
      parameters:
      - "$ref": "#/components/parameters/Id"
      - "$ref": "#/components/parameters/TenantId"
      - "$ref": "#/components/parameters/VendorId"
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Tenant details
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                "$ref": "#/components/schemas/Tenant"
        '404':
          "$ref": "#/components/responses/NotFound"
    patch:
      tags:
      - Tenants
      summary: Update tenant
      operationId: updateTenant
      parameters:
      - "$ref": "#/components/parameters/Id"
      - "$ref": "#/components/parameters/TenantId"
      - "$ref": "#/components/parameters/VendorId"
      requestBody:
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              "$ref": "#/components/schemas/TenantUpdate"
      responses:
        '200':
          description: Tenant updated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                "$ref": "#/components/schemas/Tenant"
        '404':
          "$ref": "#/components/responses/NotFound"
        '422':
          "$ref": "#/components/responses/UnprocessableEntity"
    put:
      tags:
      - Tenants
      summary: Update tenant
      operationId: replaceTenant
      parameters:
      - "$ref": "#/components/parameters/Id"
      - "$ref": "#/components/parameters/TenantId"
      - "$ref": "#/components/parameters/VendorId"
      requestBody:

content.text:
Resolving the schemas conflict: keeping User/UserCreate/UserUser from the feature in expanded form and removing duplicate compact Invite blocks — preserving the later expanded Invite definitions.

[REDACTED]

input.new_string:
schemas:
    User:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: integer
        email:
          type: string
          format: email
        cognito_sub:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        updated_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
    UserCreate:
      type: object
      required:
      - user
      properties:
        user:
          type: object
          required: [name]
          properties:
            email:
              type: string
              format: email
            cognito_sub:
              type: string
    UserUpdate:
      type: object
      properties:
        user:
          type: object
          properties:
            email:
              type: string
              format: email
            cognito_sub:
              type: string
    Tenant:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: integer
        created_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        name:
          type: string
        org_id:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        tenant_type:
          type: string
          enum: [org, vendor]
        updated_at:
          type: string
          format: date-time
        vendor_id:
          type: string
          nullable: true
    TenantCreate:
      type: object
      required:
      - tenant
      properties:
        tenant:
          type: object
          required: [tenant_type, name]
          properties:
            tenant_type:
              type: string
              enum: [org, vendor]
            name:
              type: string
            org_id:
              type: string
              nullable: true
            vendor_id:
              type: string
              nullable: true
    TenantUpdate:
      type: object
      properties:
        tenant:
          type: object
          properties:
            tenant_type:
              type: string
              enum: [org, vendor]
            name:
              type: string
            org_id:
              type: string
              nullable: true
            vendor_id:
              type: string
              nullable: true
    TenantMembership:

input.command: cd /Users/duhl/git/anedot_next && ruby -e "require 'yaml'; YAML.load_file('doc/openapi.yaml'); puts 'YAML OK'" && git add app/controllers/application_controller.rb doc/openapi.yaml Gemfile.lock && git rebase --continue

input.description: Verify clean rebase and latest commit

content.text:
Here’s what was conflicted and how it was resolved:

### `app/controllers/application_controller.rb`
- **Kept `main`’s `VendorTenantLink` flow** (secure vendor → client access).
- **Switched the link lookup to `client_tenant_id: tenant_id`** so both `tenants/:tenant_id/...` and `.../tenants/:id` work (the previous `main` hunk only used `params[:tenant_id]`).
- Dropped the older `client_tenants.find` approach.

### `Gemfile.lock`
- **Reset to `main`’s lockfile**, then ran **`bundle install`** so the lockfile matches the current `Gemfile` (including `rack-cors`).

### `doc/openapi.yaml`
- **POST `/tenants`**: `operationId: tenantsCreate` and the **TenantId + VendorId** query params, matching the rest of the spec and `set_tenant` / vendor scoping.
- **GET/PATCH/PUT `/tenants/{id}`**: **main’s** structure (tags, `tenantsShow` / `replaceTenant`, all three query params) plus a **PATCH** block aligned with `resources :tenants, :update` (`operationId: updateTenant`, same body/responses as PUT).
- **`components/schemas`**: **User** (with `cognito_sub` and email format) / **UserCreate** / **UserUpdate**; **Tenant** and create/update with **`tenant_type` enum `org` | `vendor`**, `vendor_id`/`org_id` as nullable strings, and **one** `Invite` block (no duplicate from the other side of the merge).

`ruby -e` YAML load passes, and **`git rebase --continue` finished** — you’re on `AN-18-payments` at `aabc8e3` with a clean work tree.

**Note:** The first `rebase --continue` likely blocked on the editor; if that ever happens in your environment, you can use `GIT_EDITOR=true git rebase --continue` (or set `core.editor`).

If you want the OpenAPI `doc` to also list **`/users` and `users/me`** (routes exist; the conflict didn’t add paths), say so and we can add those next.

[REDACTED]
